questions.question_text: Coral reefs are described as 'spectacular' natural wonders in the ocean. What does 'spectacular' mean?

options.text: Ordinary and commonly found in most parts of the ocean
Strikingly impressive and remarkable in appearance
Extremely small and very difficult to see clearly
Dangerous and unsafe for humans to approach

questions.explanation_ar: spectacular تعني مذهل ورائع، أي شيء يثير الإعجاب الشديد بمظهره أو حجمه أو تأثيره.

questions.question_text: Coral reefs 'constitute' less than one percent of the ocean floor. What does 'constitute' mean?

options.text: To destroy and permanently remove something from an area
To surround and protect the outer edges of something
To make up or form a particular portion of a larger whole
To measure and calculate the total size of something

questions.explanation_ar: constitute تعني يُشكّل أو يكوّن، أي أن يمثّل شيء ما جزءاً أو نسبة من كلٍّ أكبر.

questions.question_text: Coral reefs are extremely 'vulnerable' to rising water temperatures. What does 'vulnerable' mean?

options.text: Easily harmed or damaged when exposed to threatening conditions
Strong and capable of resisting all types of environmental damage
Well prepared and fully adapted to survive any kind of change
Too large and complex to be seriously affected by minor changes

questions.explanation_ar: vulnerable تعني عرضة للخطر أو ضعيف، أي أن الشيء يمكن إلحاق الضرر به بسهولة عند مواجهة التهديدات.

questions.question_text: Coral reefs 'thrive' in warm, shallow waters with plenty of sunlight. What does 'thrive' mean?

options.text: To struggle and slowly disappear from an area over time
To remain in one place without growing or developing
To produce food through the process of photosynthesis only
To grow and develop very successfully in favourable conditions

questions.explanation_ar: thrive تعني يزدهر وينمو بشكل قوي، أي أن الكائن الحي يتطور ويتقدم بنجاح عندما تتوفر الظروف المناسبة له.

questions.question_text: Coral and algae share a 'mutual' partnership that benefits both organisms. What does 'mutual' mean?

options.text: Competitive, where one side always benefits more than the other
Shared and beneficial to both sides of a relationship equally
Temporary and expected to end naturally after a short period
One-sided, with only one partner receiving any real benefits

questions.explanation_ar: mutual تعني متبادل ومشترك، أي أن كلا الطرفين يستفيد من العلاقة بشكل متساوٍ وفي الوقت ذاته.

questions.question_text: In scientific and geographical contexts, 'spans' refers to the distance or period over which something extends.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. spans تعني يمتد عبر، وتصف المسافة الجغرافية أو الفترة الزمنية التي يغطيها شيء ما.

questions.question_text: When algae growth 'overwhelms' a coral reef, it means the reef has successfully recovered and become healthier.
questions.explanation_ar: خطأ. overwhelm تعني يطغى على ويغلب، أي أن المشكلة تتجاوز القدرة على المقاومة، وهو عكس التعافي.

questions.question_text: New scientific methods have _____ to help researchers study coral reef ecosystems more effectively.
questions.explanation_ar: emerged تعني ظهرت أو برزت، وتُستخدم للتعبير عن ظهور شيء جديد أو أسلوب مبتكر بشكل تدريجي.

questions.question_text: Which sentence uses the present perfect continuous correctly?

options.text: Scientists have study coral reefs for many years.
The water temperature has rising for the past decade.
Marine biologists have been monitoring coral health since 2010.
Divers been studying the reef conditions for three weeks.

questions.explanation_ar: تكوين المضارع التام المستمر هو: have/has + been + فعل-ing. الخيار c هو الوحيد الصحيح، بينما تخطئ الخيارات الأخرى في حذف been أو في صيغة الفعل.

questions.question_text: Complete: 'The scientists _____ coral bleaching for the past six months.' (focus on ongoing research)

options.text: have studied
have been studying
have been studied
studied

questions.explanation_ar: نستخدم المضارع التام المستمر (have been studying) للتركيز على استمرارية البحث خلال ستة أشهر. المضارع التام البسيط (have studied) يُبرز النتيجة فقط، والماضي البسيط (studied) يدل على انتهاء الفعل.

questions.question_text: Choose the correct option: 'The reef has been changing _____ the water temperature rose sharply.'

options.text: since
for
when
during

questions.explanation_ar: نستخدم since مع المضارع التام المستمر للإشارة إلى نقطة بداية زمنية محددة (since + حدث/وقت)، بينما for تُستخدم مع فترة زمنية كاملة (for six months).

questions.question_text: Which sentence best expresses an action that started in the past and is still happening now?

options.text: Scientists discovered coral bleaching as a problem in the 1980s.
The algae left the coral when water temperatures suddenly increased.
The reef has been losing coral cover gradually for several decades.
Researchers will be studying reef recovery throughout next year.

questions.explanation_ar: المضارع التام المستمر (has been losing) يُعبّر عن فعل مستمر بدأ في الماضي ولا يزال قائماً، بخلاف الماضي البسيط المنتهي أو المستقبل المستمر.

questions.question_text: 'Dr. Al-Rashid has been researching Red Sea corals since she joined the marine institute' is grammatically correct.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. تُستخدم صيغة have been + فعل-ing مع since لتعبّر عن نشاط بدأ في وقت محدد في الماضي ولا يزال مستمراً حتى الآن.

questions.question_text: Match each sentence beginning to the time expression that correctly completes it.

left_items: Marine biologists have been studying this reef _____ twenty years.
The coral has been bleaching _____ the heatwave began last summer.
The divers have been _____ reef health every morning.
The temperature has been _____ steadily for months.

correct_answer.left: Marine biologists have been studying this reef _____ twenty years.
The coral has been bleaching _____ the heatwave began last summer.
The divers have been _____ reef health every morning.
The temperature has been _____ steadily for months.

questions.explanation_ar: for تُستخدم مع فترة زمنية (twenty years)، وsince مع نقطة بداية (the heatwave began)، وmonitoring وrising هما الأفعال الصحيحة للمضارع التام المستمر.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, what percentage of all marine species do coral reefs support?

options.text: Less than 1%
Approximately 25%
About 50%
More than 75%

questions.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن الشعاب المرجانية تدعم حوالي 25% من جميع الأنواع البحرية رغم أنها تُشكّل أقل من 1% من قاع المحيط.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, what happens to coral during a bleaching event caused by rising temperatures?

options.text: The coral grows more rapidly and changes to a bright orange colour
The coral produces extra algae to compensate for the increased heat
The coral sinks deeper to find cooler and less stressful water
The coral expels its algae and turns white, possibly dying if conditions persist

questions.explanation_ar: وصف النص أن ارتفاع درجات حرارة الماء يجعل المرجان يطرد طحالبه التكافلية فيتحول إلى اللون الأبيض، وقد يموت إذا لم تتحسن الأوضاع.

questions.question_text: Passage B says Red Sea corals survive at 32°C while most corals die at 29°C. What does this best suggest?

options.text: Red Sea corals have developed unique heat-resistance not found in other reefs
Red Sea corals are smaller and less biologically complex than other corals
The Red Sea is the coldest ocean region where coral reefs can survive
Scientists have artificially raised Red Sea temperatures to study coral reactions

questions.explanation_ar: الفارق في تحمّل الحرارة (32°C مقابل 29°C) يكشف أن شعاب البحر الأحمر طوّرت تكيّفات مقاومة فريدة لا تمتلكها معظم شعاب العالم.

questions.question_text: According to Passage B, Dr. Al-Rashid's team uses underwater drones to monitor reef health across large areas.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. ذكر النص أن فريق الدكتورة الراشد يستخدم طائرات مسيّرة تحت الماء لرصد صحة الشعاب المرجانية عبر مناطق شاسعة.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, the Great Barrier Reef in Australia stretches over _____ kilometers in length.
questions.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن الحاجز المرجاني العظيم في أستراليا يمتد على مسافة تزيد على 2,300 كيلومتر.

questions.question_text: Match each threat to coral reefs from Passage A to its direct effect on the ecosystem.

right_items: excess algae growth that overwhelms coral
weakening of coral calcium carbonate skeletons

correct_answer.right: excess algae growth that overwhelms coral
weakening of coral calcium carbonate skeletons

questions.explanation_ar: ربط النص كل تهديد بأثره: الحرارة تسبب الابيضاض، ومخلفات الزراعة تعزز الطحالب، والصيد الجائر يخل السلاسل الغذائية، والحموضة تضعف هياكل المرجان الكلسية.
